localBlocks.name: accordion
localBlocks.description: Expandable details/summary sections for FAQ and collapsible content

localBlocks.name: before-after
localBlocks.description: Before/after image comparison with slider navigation dots. Multiple pairs create a carousel.

localBlocks.name: cards
localBlocks.description: Grid of items with image and text body (ul/li pattern)

localBlocks.name: carousel
localBlocks.description: Rotating slide content with navigation dots and prev/next buttons

localBlocks.name: clinic-finder
localBlocks.description: Interactive clinic search with Google Maps integration. Configuration-style block with key-value rows.

localBlocks.name: footer
localBlocks.description: Site footer loaded as a fragment from /footer path

localBlocks.name: fragment
localBlocks.description: Reusable content section loaded from a path via .plain.html

localBlocks.name: header
localBlocks.description: Site header with logo, primary nav links, dropdown FAQ menu, and clinic-finder CTA

localBlocks.name: hero
localBlocks.description: Full-width hero with background image, H1, subtext, optional CTA. Has video variant for Vimeo backgrounds.

localBlocks.name: metadata
localBlocks.description: Configuration block that reads authored key-value pairs and sets document meta tags

localBlocks.name: tabs
localBlocks.description: Accessible tabbed interface with ARIA roles for switching content panels

localBlocks.name: topic-menu
localBlocks.description: Sticky horizontal anchor navigation for FAQ page. Highlights active section via IntersectionObserver scrollspy.

blockCollectionAvailable.name: video
blockCollectionAvailable.note: Available in Block Collection. Local hero block has a video variant which covers the one video-embed use case. Standalone video block may be needed if video appears outside hero context.

blockCollectionAvailable.name: quote
blockCollectionAvailable.note: No testimonial/quote patterns found on juvederm.nl.

blockPartyReferences.name: Image-Compare
blockPartyReferences.description: Simple before/after image comparison with slider overlay
blockPartyReferences.relevance: Alternative to local before-after block. Local implementation is preferred — it has Dutch labels ('Voor'/'Na'), multi-pair carousel support, and Juvederm-specific styling.

blockPartyReferences.name: Interactive Map Implementation
blockPartyReferences.description: Google Maps API with category-based filtering, marker clustering, and responsive design
blockPartyReferences.relevance: Reference for clinic-finder Google Maps integration. Local clinic-finder block uses a simpler config-driven approach with search-by-city, more appropriate for this pharma site.

blockPartyReferences.name: Video block
blockPartyReferences.description: AEM assets plugin video block with poster image and Dynamic Media support
blockPartyReferences.relevance: Potential reference for treatment page video embeds. Local hero (video) variant covers primary use case. May adopt if standalone video blocks become needed.

blockPartyReferences.name: Responsive Slider
blockPartyReferences.description: Swiper.js-based responsive slider with centralized configuration management
blockPartyReferences.relevance: Alternative carousel approach. Local carousel block (from Block Collection) is simpler and doesn't add Swiper.js dependency.

contentPatternMapping.description: Full-width teaser with background image, H1 title, optional description and CTA button

cells: H1 heading, paragraph text, CTA link (combined in one cell)

contentModel.notes: Single-column layout. Row 1 is the background image, Row 2 contains heading + text + optional CTA.

variants.name: hero (video)
variants.description: Video background from Vimeo URL instead of image

contentPatternMapping.description: Grid of 3-4 benefit items, each with H3/H4 heading and supporting paragraph text

contentModel.notes: Standard columns block. Each column contains an H3 heading followed by supporting text. Variant 'highlights' adds visual emphasis styling.

variants.name: columns (highlights)
variants.description: Visually emphasized benefit columns with brand primary color accents

contentPatternMapping.description: Carousel of JUVÉDERM® product cards with product image, H4 title, and body text

cells: product image (picture) | H4 product name + paragraph description

contentModel.notes: Each row is one carousel slide. Col 1 = product image, Col 2 = product name (H4) + description paragraph. 5-6 slides per treatment page.

variants.name: carousel (product-cards)
variants.description: Card-styled carousel slides for product showcase

contentPatternMapping.description: Before/after treatment comparison with two images and interactive slider control

contentModel.notes: Each row = one before/after pair. Col 1 = before image, Col 2 = after image. Multiple rows produce a carousel with navigation dots. Labels 'Voor'/'Na' are added by JS.

contentPatternMapping.occurrenceCount: 6+ uses (5 on homepage inside tabs, 1 per treatment page)

customBlockSpec.why-custom: No Block Collection equivalent. Block Party 'Image-Compare' lacks multi-pair carousel, Dutch localization, and pharma brand styling integration.

contentPatternMapping.description: VROUWELIJK (Female) / MANNELIJK (Male) tab switcher containing treatment area content with image maps and descriptions

cells: Tab label: VROUWELIJK | Tab content: treatment area cards (image + H3 + paragraph + link)
Tab label: MANNELIJK | Tab content: treatment area cards (image + H3 + paragraph + link)

contentModel.notes: Standard tabs block. Each row = one tab. Col 1 = tab label, Col 2 = tab panel content. Content within each tab panel can include cards sub-block or styled default content with treatment area descriptions.

contentPatternMapping.description: Interactive clinic search with Google Maps, search by city/postcode

cells: Vind een kliniek bij jou in de buurt
Voer je postcode of stad in

contentModel.notes: Configuration block with key-value pairs. JS reads config, creates search UI, initializes Google Maps. Actual clinic data loaded dynamically via API.

variants.name: clinic-finder (full-page)
variants.description: Full-page layout with prominent map, used on /find-a-clinic

variants.name: clinic-finder (compact)
variants.description: Inline compact version as CTA widget on content pages

customBlockSpec.blockPartyRef: Interactive Map by meejain (generic Google Maps, lacks clinic-specific search UX)
customBlockSpec.why-custom: Requires Allergan/AbbVie clinic API integration, Google Maps with Places Autocomplete, and pharma-brand search UX. No reusable block covers this specific pattern.

contentPatternMapping.description: Expandable FAQ items with question as summary and answer as body content

contentModel.notes: Standard accordion pattern. Row per item. Col 1 = clickable question label (rendered as <summary>). Col 2 = expandable answer body. Treatment pages have 3-5 items; FAQ page has sections with many items.

variants.name: accordion (faq)
variants.description: FAQ-specific styling with brand colors and larger text

contentPatternMapping.description: Bottom-of-page accordion serving as footer navigation with grouped link lists (Behandeling, FAQ sections)

structure.description: Footer fragment (/footer) contains: accordion block with nav groups, legal links bar, social links, copyright notice

contentModel.notes: The AEM <footer> element is empty — the actual footer content is an accordion + legal links bar rendered above the AEM footer. In EDS, this becomes the /footer fragment containing an accordion block + default content for legal/social/copyright.

contentPatternMapping.description: Required Dutch pharmaceutical disclaimer: warning icon + 'Kijk uit. Jezelf mooier maken kan lelijk uitpakken.' text

structure.description: Section with style 'pharma-notice' containing: warning icon image + H3 disclaimer heading + paragraph explanation text

contentModel.notes: No block needed. Authored as default content within a section styled as 'pharma-notice'. The section style adds the background color, icon positioning, and branded warning appearance. This is a legal/regulatory compliance requirement for Dutch pharma advertising.

structure.description: Plain Vimeo URL in content auto-blocks as embed. For hero context, use hero (video) variant.

contentModel.notes: Sparse usage — only some treatment pages. Vimeo URLs can be auto-blocked by scripts.js. If standalone video display is needed, the Block Collection 'video' block is available. The local hero block already handles video backgrounds.

contentPatternMapping.description: Scroll-triggered reveal animation on homepage content sections

structure.description: Not a block. CSS animation triggered by IntersectionObserver in scripts.js. Applied via section style 'scroll-reveal'.

contentModel.notes: Pure presentational effect. Implemented as a section style that triggers fade-in/slide-up animation when content scrolls into viewport. No authoring table needed.

contentPatternMapping.description: Footnotes and reference citations with superscript numbers (pharma compliance)

structure.description: Section with style 'references' containing small-text paragraphs with citation numbers

contentModel.notes: No block needed. Authored as default content (paragraphs with superscript reference numbers) within a section styled as 'references'. The section style reduces font size, adds muted color, and compact spacing. Essential for pharma regulatory compliance.

contentPatternMapping.description: Sticky horizontal anchor navigation for FAQ page topic sections with scrollspy highlighting

cells: unordered list of anchor links (#about-juvederm, #duration, #results, etc.)

contentModel.notes: Single-cell block containing a list of anchor links. JS makes it sticky on scroll and highlights the active section via IntersectionObserver. Only used on the FAQ page.

customBlockSpec.why-custom: No Block Collection equivalent for sticky anchor navigation with scrollspy. Simple enough to build from scratch.

blocksFromCollection.name: hero
blocksFromCollection.purpose: Page hero with background image + heading + CTA

customBlocks.name: clinic-finder
customBlocks.purpose: Clinic search with Google Maps integration

customBlocks.name: topic-menu
customBlocks.purpose: Sticky anchor navigation for FAQ page

customBlocks.name: metadata
customBlocks.purpose: Configuration block for document meta tags

defaultContent.reason: Simple icon + text, no interactive behavior needed
Plain text footnotes, no structural complexity
CSS-only animation, not a content pattern

sectionStyles.name: pharma-notice
sectionStyles.description: Pharmaceutical disclaimer section with warning icon and branded background
cssFeatures: Distinct visual emphasis for regulatory compliance

sectionStyles.name: references
sectionStyles.description: Footnotes/citations section with small text and compact spacing

sectionStyles.name: scroll-reveal
sectionStyles.description: Scroll-triggered fade-in/slide-up animation for content sections

sectionStyles.name: highlight
sectionStyles.description: Visually emphasized content section with brand background color

sharedSections.name: treatment-tabs
sharedSections.recommendation: Author as a fragment and include via fragment block to avoid duplication

sharedSections.name: clinic-finder-cta
sharedSections.description: Compact clinic finder widget as page CTA
sharedSections.recommendation: Author as a fragment for the compact variant; full-page version is standalone

sharedSections.name: pharma-notice
sharedSections.recommendation: Author as a fragment since it's identical on all pages and legally required

noteablePatterns: All 5 treatment pages follow same template
Simple text-only page with contact info
Long-form text content, no interactive components

externalDependencies.notes: Loaded only on pages with clinic-finder block. Includes Places Autocomplete.
Backend API for clinic search results. Endpoint TBD during implementation.
Brand typography. Consider self-hosting for performance.

implementationPriority.reason: Used on all content pages, critical for visual fidelity
Used for FAQ and footer navigation on every page
Benefits grid appears on homepage and all treatment pages
Product cards on all treatment pages
Grid pattern used within tabs content
Custom — treatment comparison slider on 6+ pages
Custom — high complexity, Google Maps dependency
Custom — only used on FAQ page
